Now that we have enabled Role and Group mapping, all access to AEM can be managed directly from your Corporate Identity Provider (in this case, Auth0). If I would like to give someone access to AEM, I just ensure that user has been added to the appropriate Role in my Corporate Identity Provider.

Additional Reading

Configuring Group Management in Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

entry.title: New SAP Architecture Center: Foundation for Enterprise Architecture & Architects
entry.content:
The new Introduction from SAP, the  SAP Architecture Center is among one of the best addition to SAP's resources, making a centralized hub for SAP solution reference architectures. Through this addition, businesses can adopt SAP Solutions in a more effective manner, extracting valuable business insights from organizational data.

 

Key Features of the SAP Architecture Center

  • Comprehensive Reference Architectures: The Architecture Center provides a wide range of reference solution architectures, covering multiple business needs though technological domains and design patterns.
  • Technology Domains: It caters various domains which include Automation, Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Enterprise Integration, Governance and Security.
  • Technology Partners: SAP Architecture Center includes architecture involving major technology players like GCP, Microsoft Azure, AWS etc.
  • Interactive Exploration Tools: SAP Architecture Center users will be able to navigate the architecture through using tools like Overview, Architecture Explorer, Card-Style, and Navigator Style Overview
  • Community Engagement: For community interaction, there is a dedicated section, encouraging collaboration and knowledge sharing among the SAP professionals.

 

SAP Viewpoints

 

Architecture Explorer:

Through the architecture explorer you navigate through the Technology Domains like integrations, AI, App Development & more and Technology Partners like AWS, Azure and GCP, discovering solution blueprints based on your specific requirement like you are  planning an AI Driven use case on Azure or exploring data Architecture AWS, the Architecture Center guides through the most inline architecture assets with ease.

 

Technology Domains:

  • Application Development & Automation: Build and extend SAP applications using low-code tools, CAP, and event-driven architectures.

  • Artificial Intelligence: Integrate AI and machine learning into business processes with SAP AI Core and Launchpad.

  • Data & Analytics: Design unified data strategies with SAP Datasphere, BW/4HANA, and Analytics Cloud.

  • Integration: Enable secure and scalable integration across cloud and on-premise systems using SAP Integration Suite.

  • Operations & Security: Implement best practices for system security, observability, DevOps, and compliance.

 

Technology Domains:

The SAP Architecture Center focuses on the reference architectures across leading hyperscalers

  • Microsoft Azure
  • AWS
  • GCP

Even if you are using GCP for orchestrating analytics workloads, running BTP services on Azure or data Pipelines on AWS. The SAP Architecture center provides architectures patterns for each environment.

 

Reference Architectures:

SAP Architecture Center contains a rich repository of reference Architectures aligned with the recommended practices, well structured and reusable blueprints that elaborates how SAP Solutions are developed, integrated, deployed and scaled real world Enterprise Scenarios. Reference Architectures within the SAP Architecture Center is formulated in key sections, enriching the overall architecture in a more organized and easy to understand way. 


Conclusion:

Looking ahead, SAP Architecture Center will play a vital role driving standardized and future scoped solutions. As all the technologies are going through a continuous evolving process and towards a more modular, cloud native and AI Integrated solutions, and SAP heading towards the same,  many more things would be part of the SAP Architecture Center in the coming days which will accelerate digital transformations, ensuring architectural consistency. By adopting these reference architectures, your organization can future proof their Digital Transformation and IT Strategy with reduced complexity.

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite CodeJam roadshow in India
entry.content:
¡Hola SAP Developers!

I've got some exciting news to share with you. We are organising an SAP Integration Suite CodeJam roadshow in India!

I understand that in July, it is still season for the king of fruits in India (🥭 mango, obviously), and my colleague @ajay_soreng and I ( @ajmaradiaga ) will go on the hunt for the best mango variety in India... while we do a few CodeJams on our roadshow 😋.  We will have three stops on the roadshow from July 14th to 18th, 2025, doing a total of four CodeJams, one in Mumbai, two in Hyderabad, and one more in Bengaluru :-).

 

Connecting systems and services using SAP Integration Suite

This SAP CodeJam instance will be about SAP Integration Suite and how we can use it to connect systems and services in our landscape.

Event-driven integration with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

This SAP CodeJam instance will be about event-driven architectures focusing on SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. During the CodeJam, we will cover different technologies/tools: SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, SAP Cloud Integration, SAP Business Application Studio, CloudEvents, and Node.js.

SAP CodeJam events part of the roadshow (Register Now!)

Below is a listing of the SAP CodeJam events on the roadshow schedule:

Dates

CodeJam topic

Location

Connecting systems and services using SAP Integration Suite

Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh #1

Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh #2

Connecting systems and services using SAP Integration Suite

Make sure to register to secure your place. It is likely that the places available will go fast. See you all somewhere in India on the roadshow!

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh Blog Collection
entry.content:
Introduction

In today’s fast-paced, event-driven world, seamless data movement and real-time responsiveness have become critical to modern application architectures. SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh (AEM) offers a powerful solution—enabling dynamic, reliable, and scalable event streaming across hybrid and multi-cloud environments.

Over time, we've created a substantial amount of content around Advanced Event Mesh—now it makes sense to bring all the pieces together in one place as a cohesive blog collection, including Guides & contributions from other AEM Experts in the Community.

This blog serves as a central hub—a curated collection of insightful articles, technical deep dives and best practices focused on AEM and SAP Event Enablement Options like RAP, AIFAEM and the SAP Event-Enablement Add-On (ASAPIO). Whether you're just getting started or looking to optimize a production-grade deployment, this collection is designed to guide you through the concepts and capabilities of AEM.

Let’s explore how event mesh technology is transforming integration strategies and empowering developers and architects to build the next generation of event-driven systems.

 

Getting Started

Get Started with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

Advanced Event Mesh: Basic Concepts 

Advanced Event Mesh: Create your first event broker

 

AEM Features

SAP Advanced Event Mesh – Manage Advance Event Mesh with API’s 

Automating Advanced Event Mesh Broker Creation 

Advanced Event Mesh: Replay

Advanced Event Mesh: Queue 

Enabling In-Order processing with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh Partitioned Queues

Securing SAP Advanced Event Mesh: OAuth Configuration and SEMP API Integration

 

Connectivity & Auth

Advanced Event Mesh: Client Authentication - Basic / OAuth 

Advanced Event Mesh Connectors and Easy Event-Driven Example of S/4HANA with Amazon S3 Integration

 

Event Portal / Developer Hub

Enable SAP Developer Hub as central Event Catalog

Discover S/4HANA Cloud Events in SAP Developer Hub

 

Monitoring

Configure SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh Distributed Tracing

Evaluate SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh Datadog Distributed Tracing

Advanced Event Mesh:Troubleshooting

 

User Management

Introduction to Group Management in SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh

Streamlining User Management in Advanced Event Mesh with SAP CIS and Identity Federation

SAP Advanced Event Mesh – Event Access Approval Workflow 

 

Deployment Options

Setting Up a Private SAP Advanced Event Mesh Cluster on SUSE Rancher RKE2

 

Kafka Bridge

SAP Integration Suite – Connect Everything FEATURING Kafka

Bridging Advanced Event Mesh and Kafka: Dynamic Topics and Headers in Action

 

SAP Event Enablement

RAP

RAP Business Events with SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh and SAP S/4HANA On Premise 2023

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [1]: Connecting AEM to S/4HANA On Premise 2023

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [2]: Creating custom business events

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [3]: Creating events with deep payloads

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [4]: Creating a derived event

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [5]: Consuming a local business event

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [6]: Consuming a remote event

 

AIFAEM

A Natural Choice: SAP AIF can now expose events to Advanced Event Mesh to power your EDA 

How to set up enhanced Events for Advanced Event Mesh with Application Interface Framework & AIFAEM

How to send out IDocs to Advanced Event Mesh using SAP Application Interface Framework & AIFAEM

How to set up Filters in AIFAEM

How to Display the JSON Payload in AIFAEM

How to send Custom HTTP Headers in AIFAEM

 

Initial Loads

Blog Series - Intitial Loads to SAP Advanced Event Mesh

 

Other

BTP Event Mesh Advance Concepts

 

Summary

This collection brings together key insights and technical deep dives around Advanced Event Mesh to help you navigate, implement, and optimize event-driven architectures. If you notice that a relevant blog post or important topic is missing, feel free to let me know in the comments—I'm happy to expand the list and keep this collection as complete and useful as possible.

*Disclaimer: The schedule is subject to alterations without notice, including potential delays or the integration of changes in subsequent update windows. It doesn't establish a commitment, promise, or legal duty to deliver any material, code, or functionality. Looking into the remaining quarters of 2025, updates will focus on quarterly content maintenance and a steady extension of product coverage. No substantial UI updates are planned for the time being.

 

The Readiness@Scale team within SAP Enterprise Support is driven to ensure release readiness across the SAP Enterprise Support portfolio. Our mission is to empower SAP customers to effectively adopt and utilize innovations.

If you have feedback or specific requests for content within the release navigator, feel free to contact us.

Don't forget to explore our team’s blogs tagged under Readiness@Scale.

Happy navigating!

Readiness@Scale  
SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: Open Connectors via APIM:
entry.content:
1. Create an API Provider for Open connectors Service Now instance using Type “Open Connectors”

 

  1. Create a proxy using API provider created in step1

No policies created on API Proxy, as we are going to pass Authorization on header in postman

  1. Create a product & add API proxy to the product

  1. Create an application in developer HUB with product created in step3 & fetch APP key & Secret for authentication purpose.

 

Testing:

  1. Use the PROXY endpoint along with the resource path to call. Use the API key & secret as authentication. Use the authorization in header for Open connectors

  1. Get incidents from Open connector Instance of Service Now:

Try to fetch the same from Postman as well using the suffix part of the URL to call incidents resource (/incidents)

Regards,

Rohini Potham

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite Basic Trial
entry.content:
Ready to Transform Your Integration Strategy?

Kick off your 30-day SAP Integration Suite Basic Trial and follow our step-by-step guide to seamlessly connect your hybrid landscape—including SAP S/4HANA Cloud and 3rd-party cloud-based databases.

  • Build a Digital Integration Hub on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)
  • Leverage advanced integration services to unify diverse systems
  • Apply the Integration Solution Advisory Methodology (ISAM) to model, manage, and adapt APIs with ease

With clear, guided instructions, you’ll experience how to streamline application, data, and process integration across your ecosystem.

  1. Sign up for a free Basic Trial and follow the easy registration steps.
  2. Access the guided workbook to walk through real-world use cases.
  3. Publish applications and test APIs in SAP Developer Hub .

The architecture

The architecture is designed to retrieve and consolidate maintenance orders associated with products that have been purchased. The overall objective is to enable a unified view of maintenance obligations linked to each product, regardless of the system in which they were generated or processed.

  • Source System - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Product purchase orders are initially processed within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
  • PostgreSQL Application Database - Maintenance Data

The step-by-step guide covers the following important topics:

Integration Assessment Overview

Implement the Integration Solution Advisory Methodology to support the definition of the integration landscape. It helps to define integration patterns and to ensure consistent usage of the integration technology. Integration Assessment provides users with a guided approach to assess their integration strategy and recommends the appropriate technologies for their business.

Create the integration flow

Implement an integration flow that maps sales orders from SAP S4/HANA Cloud with maintenance orders from a cloud based maintenance order database

Create and deploy API

Publish, promote, and oversee APIs in a secure and scalable environment with SAP API Management. During the unit, you will secure and publish an API. This API can then be used by any developer - internal or external - in a secure way, to build integrations or applications.
 

Use SAP Developers Hub:

Implement Application Key policies:

 

Test you application deployment

 

Important:

The basic trial only offers a selected subset of the overall functionality of SAP Integration Suite. Key user capabilities and administration rights for customization, master data management, or integration are not offered in this trial.

entry.title: New Pipeline Concept features with version 1.0.12: improved handling of Custom Header Properties
entry.content:
A new version (1.0.12) of the Process Integration Pipeline package has recently been released, featuring several minor technical updates, bug fixes, and a reworked approach to handling custom header properties. This blog outlines the enhancements made in the area of custom header property processing.

Intro

Starting with version 1.0.7 of the Process Integration Pipeline package, support for custom header properties was introduced. Custom header properties enable enhanced searchability of the message processing log by allowing key-value pairs – extracted from the message payload or other relevant attributes – to be indexed and queried.

To use this feature, you must configure the customHeaderProperties header in a scenario-specific integration flow step. This header is then propagated to the generic integration flows. The header value is defined as a pipe-delimited ('|') string, where each key-value pair is separated by a colon (':'). For example:

purchaseOrder:${property.purchaseOrder}|companyCode:${property.companyCode}|vendorNumber:${property.vendorNumber}

This implementation meets basic requirements for transmitting custom header properties across multiple integration flows via a single header. However, it is somewhat error-prone due to the use of fixed delimiter characters. If any value contains reserved characters such as colons (e.g., in timestamps), parsing may fail.

Another limitation is the two-step setup required when extracting multiple key fields from an XML payload using XPath: first, each value must be stored in a message header or exchange property, and only then can they be combined into a single customHeaderProperties string.

To address these limitations, an enhanced processing option has been introduced. Instead of using a delimited string, you can define exchange properties with the prefix 'customHeader_' and invoke the Groovy script addCustomHeaderProperties. This script generates the customHeaderProperties header as a Base64-encoded JSON object, preserving all key-value pairs.

With this approach, special characters within header values – such as colons, pipes, or quotes – are fully supported and correctly handled, making the process more robust and reliable.

New processing option: Using exchange properties

For each custom header property, you can create an exchange property with prefix 'customHeader_' in a content modifier step. The name of the exchange property without the prefix becomes the name of the custom header property.

After the Content Modifier step, you need to invoke the Groovy script addCustomHeaderProperties from the script collection Pipeline Generic - Script Collection, which is part of the provided integration package. This script reads all exchange properties prefixed with 'customHeader_', constructs a JSON object from their name-value pairs, and Base64-encodes the result.

In addition to generating the customHeaderProperties header, the script also attaches these custom header values to the message processing log, ensuring they are available for monitoring and diagnostics. The resulting customHeaderProperties header is then seamlessly passed along to the generic integration flows.

In the generic integration flows, the addCustomHeaderProperties Groovy script splits the header value using the pipe character ('|') for backward compatibility. It then evaluates each resulting segment (or the original value if no pipe is present) to determine whether it is Base64-encoded. All key-value pairs extracted from the decoded or raw segments are stored in a map, which is subsequently used as the source for custom header properties.

In the message monitor, the custom headers are displayed in the Properties tab of the message processing logs of the generic integration flows. In the filter section, you can search for specific custom header values.

Previous Approach Still Supported: Manually Setting customHeaderProperties

In a content modifier step, you can create a new header customHeaderProperties. The source value of the header customHeaderProperties must follow the following pattern:

  • Name and value are separated by colon (':')
  • Name and value pairs are separated by pipe ('|')

Optionally, you can invoke the addCustomHeaderProperties script to add custom header properties to your scenario-specific flow.

At the same time, the script transforms the previously defined pipe-delimited string into a JSON object, encodes it in Base64, and updates the customHeaderProperties header accordingly: {"docType":"Z0","docRefId":"Test Doc 01"}

In the generic integration flows, the Groovy script addCustomHeaderProperties splits up the list of name and value pairs and adds the custom header properties accordingly.

In the message monitor, the custom headers are displayed in the Properties tab of the message processing logs of the generic integration flows. In the filter section, you can search for specific custom header values.

Combined approach

A combined approach is also supported: you can use the customHeaderProperties header with a pipe-delimited string, and simultaneously define exchange properties with the prefix 'customHeader_' in the same Content Modifier.

In the example below, the custom header key companyCode is included both in the delimited string and as an exchange property.

When the addCustomHeaderProperties script is invoked, it merges the entries from the customHeaderProperties header and the 'customHeader_' exchange properties into a single map. It then logs the resulting custom header properties in the message processing log and sets a new merged value – encoded as a Base64 JSON object – back to the customHeaderProperties header.

In this case, the value from the exchange property customHeader_companyCode takes precedence over the one from the delimited string. This is because the script first reads values from the existing header, then adds or overwrites them with those from the exchange properties – allowing you to override specific values if needed.

entry.title: New Integration Content for Coupa to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence Now Available
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce that new integration content for Coupa to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence is now available, offering organizations an enhanced capability to analyze their end-to-end procurement processes. This valuable integration is accessible free of charge on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, empowering businesses to make data-driven decisions with greater precision and efficiency.

Streamline Your Procurement Analysis

Procurement processes are complex, involving numerous stakeholders and steps that can create inefficiencies and gaps if not closely monitored. By leveraging the new integration content for Coupa and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, you can gain comprehensive insights into your procurement workflows. This integration helps you visualize, analyze, and optimize the entire procurement process from purchase requisition to payment, ensuring greater transparency and continuous improvement.

How to Access the Integration Content

Accessing this powerful integration is straightforward. To get started:

Log in to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

 

Navigate to the “Explore” tab and search for “SAP Signavio”.

 

Locate the new integration content for Coupa.

 

View the integration package and dedicated integration flows.

 

The integration package includes dedicated integration flows that outline the relevant business objects and events extracted by default. This setup enables a quick start with the flexibility to adjust and extend the content to meet your specific business needs.

Key Benefits of the Integration

  • End-to-End Visibility: Gain a holistic view of your procurement operations, identifying bottlenecks and opportunities for optimization.

  • Customizable Data Extraction: The predefined business objects and events can be tailored to your organization’s unique requirements, offering adaptability for varied use cases.

  • Enhanced Decision-Making: With seamless data extraction from Coupa and integration into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, organizations can make informed decisions based on accurate process execution data.

Share Your Feedback

We value your input and look forward to hearing your thoughts on this new integration package. Share your experiences and suggestions in the comments section below.

Start analyzing and optimizing your procurement processes today with the new Coupa to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence integration. Discover how this solution can transform your operations and drive better outcomes.

In case you have not seen it yet, also check out the integration content we have recently released for Salesforce (Blog post / Integration content) and Microsoft Dynamics CRM (Blog post / Integration content)
